summary: Telegram bot exfiltration channel in main module
details: Static analysis (a static pattern match) flagged index.js lines 60-63 containing api.telegram.org/bot URLs with sendMessage and chat_id= parameters. This is the canonical pattern for using Telegram's Bot API as a data exfiltration channel — attackers favor it because it bypasses most egress filtering (TLS to a legitimate service) and requires no attacker-controlled infrastructure. The package name '0e' is a meaningless two-character name with no legitimate reason to integrate Telegram messaging in its main module. the analysis produced no findings to contextualize as benign (e.g., a documented chat bot library). Absent contradicting evidence that this is an intentional Telegram bot SDK, the pattern is consistent with credential/data exfiltration.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
